title: Unified Payroll Business Rules Catalogue — BEST GROUP
description: Consolidated, machine-readable payroll rules for NirvaCore, grounded in Thai law (Labour Protection Act B.E. 2541 & amendments, Social Security Act, Revenue Code) and BEST GROUP's reality: a large, multi-site, OT-heavy service workforce that is a MIX of daily-paid (รายวัน) and monthly-paid (รายเดือน) staff. Supersedes and expands the 7 starter PAY-* rules in business_rules.json (see `crosswalk`).
scope_notes: applies_to: daily | monthly | all — Thailand treats รายวัน and รายเดือน differently for holiday pay, so every earning rule states which it covers.
All statutory_value figures are marked [verify] — Thai rates/thresholds/dates change. NirvaCore MUST hold them as DATED, CONFIGURABLE parameters keyed to effective_date, never hard-coded.
severity: block (hard stop) | warn | info. rule_type: validation | calculation | control | policy | filing.
This is a management/engineering artifact, not legal advice. Confirm against MoL / RD / SSO / a Thai labour lawyer before reliance.

parameters._comment: Default statutory parameters — verify & date-stamp in NirvaCore config.

sso_rate_employee.source: Social Security Act [verify - temp reductions occur]

rules.name: Normal working hours ceiling
rules.when: scheduled normal hours exceed 8/day or 48/week (7/day for hazardous work)
rules.then: flag schedule; excess must be treated & paid as overtime, not normal time

rules.name: Overtime requires consent
rules.when: OT hours recorded without a captured employee consent for that OT
rules.then: do not auto-approve OT pay; require consent record (except statutory emergency exceptions)

rules.name: Weekly OT + holiday-work cap
rules.when: combined OT + holiday-work hours in a week exceed the statutory cap
rules.then: flag for compliance review (excess hours are a legal risk, RSK-01)

rules.name: Weekly holiday & rest period
rules.when: no weekly holiday within 6 days, or no ≥1h rest after 5 consecutive hours
rules.then: flag rostering violation; affects holiday-pay calculation

rules.name: Minimum wage floor (by province)
rules.when: effective daily wage < statutory provincial minimum wage for the work location
rules.then: BLOCK payroll; flag underpayment (RSK-01)

rules.name: Hourly rate derivation
rules.when: computing any hourly-based amount (OT, holiday)
rules.then: derive hourly rate consistently per pay type
rules.formula: monthly: hourly = salary / 30 / 8 ; daily: hourly = daily_wage / 8 [verify divisors per company policy & contract]

rules.name: Overtime on a normal working day
rules.when: hours worked beyond normal hours on a normal working day
rules.then: OT pay = hourly_rate × 1.5 × ot_hours

rules.name: Holiday work — normal hours (daily-paid)
rules.when: daily-paid employee works on a weekly/traditional holiday during normal hours
rules.then: pay an ADDITIONAL 1× the day's wage rate (total ≈ 2× for that day), since daily-paid are not normally paid on holidays

rules.name: Holiday work — normal hours (monthly-paid)
rules.when: monthly-paid employee works on a holiday during normal hours
rules.then: pay an ADDITIONAL 1× hourly for hours worked (salary already covers the holiday)

rules.name: Overtime on a holiday
rules.when: hours worked beyond normal hours on a holiday
rules.then: holiday OT = hourly_rate × 3 × ot_hours

rules.name: Allowances & site premiums
rules.when: computing gross pay with allowances (shift, skill/position, attendance/diligence, site, travel)
rules.then: add per contract; CLASSIFY each as 'wage' vs 'non-wage' — only true 'wage' (ค่าจ้าง) elements enter OT/holiday/severance base
rules.legal_source: LPA definition of ค่าจ้าง (§5)
rules.notes: Misclassifying allowances as non-wage to shrink OT/severance base is a common audit finding and legal risk.

rules.name: Service charge distribution
rules.when: a contract/site collects a service charge pooled for staff
rules.then: distribute per agreed scheme, document allocation, retain evidence

rules.name: Proration for partial period
rules.when: new hire, termination, or unpaid leave mid-period
rules.then: prorate salary by worked/paid days using the company's documented day-count basis

rules.name: Annual leave entitlement & pay
rules.when: employee has completed 1 year of service
rules.then: grant ≥6 paid working days/year; pay leave at normal wage; unused handled per policy/law

rules.name: Sick leave (paid)
rules.when: employee takes sick leave
rules.then: pay up to 30 working days/year at normal wage; require medical certificate if ≥3 consecutive days

rules.name: Personal/business leave (paid)
rules.when: employee takes necessary personal business leave
rules.then: pay ≥3 working days/year

rules.name: Maternity leave
rules.when: eligible employee takes maternity leave
rules.then: grant up to 98 days (incl. holidays & antenatal visits); employer pays wage ≤45 days; SSO covers further benefit

rules.name: Military / other statutory leave
rules.when: military service, sterilization, training, or ordination leave taken
rules.then: apply statutory paid-day limits (e.g. military ≤60 paid days)

rules.name: Social Security contribution
rules.when: computing statutory deductions
rules.then: employee 5% of wage on base 1,650-15,000 (max 750/mo); employer matches 5%; remit via SSO e-service

rules.name: PIT withholding (PND.1)
rules.when: paying salary/wages
rules.then: compute PIT withholding on annualized basis per progressive table & allowances; deduct monthly

rules.name: Provident fund (if enrolled)
rules.when: employee is a provident-fund member
rules.then: deduct employee contribution (2-15% per rules), add employer contribution, remit to fund manager

rules.name: Statutory deduction limits
rules.when: total non-tax deductions (advances, damages, savings, union, fund) would exceed limits
rules.then: BLOCK; each such deduction ≤10% and aggregate ≤20% (1/5) of wage unless employee consents; tax/SSO/court orders excepted
rules.notes: Protects workers from over-deduction; a dignity + legal control.

rules.name: Advance / loan recovery
rules.when: recovering a salary advance or loan
rules.then: recover only with consent & within §76 limits; track outstanding balance; never net to below minimum wage

rules.name: Severance pay by tenure
rules.when: employee is terminated without serious cause (not resignation/§119 cause)
rules.then: pay statutory severance per tenure band on last wage rate

rules.name: Notice / payment in lieu
rules.when: terminating without the required advance notice
rules.then: pay wages in lieu of notice (≥ one pay period / per contract)

rules.name: Final pay timing
rules.when: employment ends (termination)
rules.then: pay all wages, OT, holiday pay & severance due within 3 days of termination

rules.name: Unused annual leave payout
rules.when: employment ends with accrued unused annual leave
rules.then: pay leave wage for unused statutory (and pro-rata current-year) annual leave

rules.name: Special severance (relocation/automation)
rules.when: termination due to relocation employee won't follow, or workforce reduction from machinery/tech
rules.then: apply special severance/additional pay where statute requires

rules.name: PND.1 monthly WHT return
rules.when: after each payroll month
rules.then: file PND.1 & remit withheld PIT by the 7th of the following month (e-filing extension applies)

rules.name: SSO contribution remittance
rules.when: after each payroll month
rules.then: remit employee+employer SSO and file by the 15th of the following month (e-service)

rules.name: Provident fund remittance
rules.when: fund members exist
rules.then: remit contributions to the fund manager per the fund's deadline

rules.name: Annual PND.1 Kor & SSO wage report
rules.when: year-end
rules.then: file PND.1 Kor (annual WHT summary) and SSO annual wage report (Kor Tor 20 Kor) by their deadlines

rules.name: Workmen's Compensation Fund
rules.when: annual WCF cycle
rules.then: pay WCF (employer-only, 0.2-1% by risk class) and reconcile actual payroll

rules.name: WHT certificate issuance
rules.when: PIT withheld from an employee
rules.then: issue 50 Tawi (withholding certificate) to employee; provide annual summary

rules.name: Time-record lock before run
rules.when: payroll run initiated while any source time record is unlocked/editable
rules.then: BLOCK until records locked & approved (input integrity)

rules.name: Segregation of duties (preparer ≠ approver)
rules.when: payroll approver == preparer
rules.then: BLOCK approval; require a different authorized approver

rules.name: Verified-hours source of truth
rules.when: payroll hours sourced from anything other than verified check-in (CHECK)
rules.then: BLOCK manual hour entry without supervisor justification & audit log (anti-ghost, links RSK-08)

rules.name: Retroactive adjustment control
rules.when: a back-dated/retro adjustment to a closed period is made
rules.then: require reason code, approval, and an offsetting auditable entry; never edit a closed run in place

rules.name: Net-pay reasonableness & variance
rules.when: an employee's net pay deviates beyond threshold vs prior period without a known driver
rules.then: flag for review before disbursement (catches calc errors & fraud)

rules.name: Rounding policy
rules.when: rounding monetary results
rules.then: apply a single documented rounding rule consistently; never round down below statutory minimums

rules.name: Bank file = approved register
rules.when: generating the bank disbursement file
rules.then: file totals must reconcile to the approved payroll register; block on mismatch; dual release

rules.name: Payslip & payroll data protection
rules.when: generating/storing/sending payslips and payroll data (personal + financial)
rules.then: encrypt at rest/in transit, access on need-to-know, no payslips via open LINE/email; consent & RoPA

rules.name: Payroll record retention
rules.when: retaining payroll/employee registers and tax records
rules.then: retain per statute (e.g. employee register & wage records; tax records ~5 yrs) then dispose securely

rules.name: Headcount-to-payroll reconciliation
rules.when: closing a payroll period
rules.then: reconcile paid employees to verified active headcount (CHECK) and to client-billed headcount; explain variances (anti-ghost)

rules.name: Duplicate identity / bank account
rules.when: two employees share a national ID or bank account, or a vendor bank == employee bank
rules.then: BLOCK disbursement; escalate to audit (ghost/fraud indicator)

rules.name: Statutory remittance reconciliation
rules.when: after filings
rules.then: reconcile PND.1 / SSO / PF / WCF remitted vs deducted; flag gaps (RSK-03/04)

rules.name: Payroll evidence pack
rules.when: each closed run
rules.then: retain approved register, bank file, statutory filings & reconciliations as a single audit-ready evidence pack (EV-FINCTRL)
